headline: 'If they catch me, it's over for me': Senegal's LGBTQ+ community lives in daily fear of repression
description: In March, Senegal passed a law increasing penalties for same-sex relations, leaving LGBTQ+ people living in fear. Several described their deeply uncertain daily lives, gripped by the constant anxiety of being arrested.

headline: 'We were meant to take as many jewels as we could': The confessions of two Louvre burglars

Iran declares Hormuz strait closed, US military insists traffic flowing
description: Iran attacked Gulf countries, including Qatar, the United Arab Emirates and Oman, after US strikes in retaliation for an attack against a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

headline: 'I would do a line before service, hidden in the bathroom': How cocaine is flooding the restaurant industry
description: Cocaine at work (2/3). From fast-food joints to Michelin-starred restaurants, drug use has become as commonplace as it is alarming in an industry driven by the pursuit of performance, a toxic party culture and grueling working conditions.

headline: French women's baseballers 'not going to give up' after national team is shut down
description: The French national women's baseball team, crowned champions of Europe twice since 2019, has been suspended by the French Federation of Baseball and Softball.

headline: Simon Abkarian, star of 'De Gaulle': 'At school in Lebanon, my nickname was de Gaulle'
description: The actor and theater director plays Charles de Gaulle in the recently released two-part film. In this article, he explains how he prepared for the role of France's World War II hero.

Martine Barrat, a photographer without fear or limits
description: At 93, the artist has lost none of her vitality or curiosity. She lives at the legendary Hotel Chelsea in New York, where she remains one of the last year-round artist residents. Today, her work is the subject of a retrospective at the Rencontres d'Arles photography festival.

headline: 'You're expected to perform, but no one wants to know how you do it': Cocaine, the toxic fuel of young professionals
description: Cocaine at work (1/3). Among young professionals, the illegal and highly addictive drug is used in a relentless pursuit of performance, often shrouded in taboo and without questioning of working conditions.

headline: Is air conditioning good or bad? Join this (fake) group chat for (real) analysis
description: Each time a new heatwave hits, the question of air conditioning and its environmental impacts crops up in the public debate. Here are the key points to help you understand what is at stake.
